ShortDescription.Value: Provides detailed traffic analysis for selected road segments. Displays route lines color-coded by volume, a breakdown of travel purposes, and also highlights destinations.

Publish.LongDescription: # Traffic Spy Provides detailed traffic analysis for selected road segments. Displays route lines color-coded by volume, a breakdown of travel purposes, and also highlights destinations. ## Features: - Route Lines: Draws lines indicating the routes that each agent will take, similar to the vanilla system but with color-coding to indicate volume, and it works in combination with the various filters. - Filtering Options: Isolate one side of a two-way road, filter to specific travel purposes, or limit analysis based on agents' distance from the selected path. - Destination Visualization: Highlights destination buildings. - Travel Purpose Summary: View agent counts, grouped by their travel purpose (e.g., commuting, cargo delivery, etc). - In addition to roads and pedestrian paths, you can analyze bus stops, subway stations, train stations, and ferry stops. - When selecting a road you can exclude the pedestrians waiting at an attached bus stop, or analyze only the transit passengers. For stations, you can analyze each individual stop/platform, or all together. ## How to Use: Click the Traffic Spy icon, and then click a road/path segment, or a transit structure. ## Tips: - Except at short distances, the routes of cims are not lane-specific. - When analyzing an intersection, you can choose "Lane Data Only" to see only the routes of vehicles who have chosen specific lanes. - Bikes count as vehicles. - Check out my other mod, Radius Delete :) ## Removal: This mod is safe to remove and doesn't add data to your save files.
Publish.ChangeLog: - Now captures vehicles that are close to the selected road segment and have switched to lane-specific pathing. - You can now analyze intersections (nodes). - Switched to custom outlines so that highlighted peds are visible on selected roads/transit. - With Vehicle filter on, now we only draw routes on vehicle lanes, even if the cim's route also includes eg walking to their car. Likewise for the Pedestrian filter. Counts however will still include cims with multi-mode routes.
